behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all four files and tells the agent to use progress.OneOff with the same arguments as the local helper; the literal strings asserted in tests come directly from preserving the existing call-site arguments.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to refactor (replace local helper with progress.OneOff) but doesn't give line numbers or the exact replacement code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on progress.OneOff calls with specific arguments that must be preserved from original call sites; any correct refactor would produce these exact strings.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run go build, go vet, and go test on three packages, not just text inspection.

test_not_tautological.reason: F2P tests require actual refactor: presence of progress.OneOff calls, absence of oneOffProgress definitions, correct imports — a stub cannot satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover compilation, vet, unit tests, refactor correctness across all four files, and import cleanup per instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named by concern (test_compilation, test_unit_worker, test_pull_go_uses_progress_oneoff, etc.) with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code refactor, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction lists files but localization within them is left to the agent; no specific line numbers given.
